title: Cold Ashby V Rothwell Manor
content: What a difference a few months makes. After the disappointment of the reverse fixture earlier in the season which fell victim to the unpredictable British weather and had to be cancelled, we were determined to make amends.
This time, the weather gods smiled upon us, and we took full advantage. The performance was controlled and clinical, with our rinks working in perfect harmony.
A well-deserved victory that shows the resilience and quality of the team.

title: Carlsberg V Cold Ashby
content: Cold Ashby Secure Solid Away Win in Historical Northampton Clash
Cold Ashby Bowls Club braved the pre-match elements to secure a convincing 46-35 shot victory against Carlsberg, played at one of the town's most historic bowling venues.
Despite an unsettling pre-match downpour that threatened to slick the green, the weather cleared just in time for the opening ends and remained dry and clear throughout the contest.

Cold Ashby found their rhythm early on the historic surface, establishing control across the rinks and maintaining a steady lead for the majority of the match. Carlsberg put up a spirited fight on their home turf, but Cold Ashby’s consistency proved too much, ultimately sealing an 11 shot victory.

The standout performance of the day came from the trio of Gerry, Ollie, and Michael, who claimed the honour of highest winning rink with an excellent display of draw bowling that kept the pressure firmly on the hosts.

title: West Haddon V Cold Ashby
content: On Thursday we travelled the short distance to play at West Haddon. On a lovely summer evening all players enjoyed the match. Cold Ashby were in control for all the match, winning on three out of the four rinks. The final match score was Cold Ashby 75 West Haddon 52. For a change the winning rink was not skipped by Roger Miller. It was Damian Pidgeon with fellow team members Guy and Dean.

title: Local Derby Delight as Cold Ashby Dominate South Kilworth
content: A spectacular performance on the green saw Cold Ashby claim a decisive 79-59 victory over South Kilworth.
Our rinks showed fantastic depth today, finding their line and length early to build a comfortable cushion by the midway point.
Pedro and the rest of the rink put on a masterclass in drawing to the jack, frustrating the opposition and seeing out a wonderful win.

title: Desborough V Cold Ashby
content: Cold Ashby faced a tough away fixture against Desborough on June 9th, playing in very difficult and testing conditions.
The home team managed to settle quickly and controlled the match right from the opening ends, ultimately leading to a final score of 71-47 in favor of Desborough.
Despite the overall setback, Cold Ashby rallied to find a brilliant positive on the day, securing a fantastic rink victory led by the strong bowling of Roger Miller, Pedro Owen, and Stuart.

title: Cold Ashby v Geddington
content: Unfortunately this match was called off due to the weather.

title: Rothwell Manor V Cold Ashby
content: Unfortunately this match was called off due to the weather.

title: Bugbrooke v Cold Ashby
content: Cold Ashby traveled away to Bugbrooke on Saturday May 30th to play under incredibly hot and sultry conditions.
The younger home side adapted much better to the heavy, humid atmosphere and their own tricky green surface, which unfortunately led to a 92 to 70 overall victory for Bugbrooke.

Despite the challenging conditions and the overall defeat, there was a fantastic bright spot for the visitors: the rink consisting of Matt, Stuart, and Roger bowled beautifully to secure a well-deserved rink win for Cold Ashby.

title: Club Competitions & BBQ
content: Our scheduled opponents unfortunately had to cancel as they were unable to field a team.
However, the blank Saturday did not go to waste. The afternoon was instead used to host our club competitions and a well-attended BBQ, raising spirits and community engagement.

title: Cold Ashby V Rugby Railway
content: On a bitterly cold evening, Cold Ashby entertained one of the strongest teams in the area.
Despite the drop in temperature, the home side put up a gallant fight from the opening end.

title: Whyte Melville V Cold Ashby
content: Cold Ashby traveled to Fernie Fields on Sunday—the home of Whyte Melville Bowls Club—for a closely contested match.
Despite a forecast threatening rain, the weather held out and the game was played in good spirit.

title: Harpole V Cold Ashby
content: Cold Ashby traveled to Harpole on Saturday for a closely contested encounter.
While the final scoreline suggests a relatively tight margin, the narrative of the match was much closer than the numbers alone imply.

title: North Kilworth vs Cold Ashby
content: In a challenging away fixture, Cold Ashby fell short against a disciplined North Kilworth side.
Final Score: North Kilworth 90 – 70 Cold Ashby. Match conditions included heavy rain which made play difficult.

title: Daventry V Cold Ashby
content: That is a fantastic turnaround! Winning by 9 shots overall (84 to 75) shows a real team effort.
A huge shout-out to Chris H, Harry, and Damo for leading the charge as the winning rinks.

title: Cold Ashby V L.V.A.
content: Under a cloudless sky, Cold Ashby entertained L.V.A. in what proved to be a highly competitive afternoon.
The home team ultimately lost narrowly by 56 shots to 51, but there were several standout performances to build on.

title: Club Competition Report: The Triples Cup
content: Overview

The season kicked off in spectacular fashion as the club hosted its first official competition of the year: The Triples Cup. Players were treated to ideal conditions and enthusiastic support from members and guests.

title: Season Opener: Cold Ashby V Fleckney
content: Cold Ashby hosted Fleckney for the highly anticipated opening match of the 2026 season on a fine but cool afternoon.
The green was in excellent condition and both sides produced a competitive display.

title: Meet and Greet
content: Members of Cold Ashby Bowls Club held their pre-season get together on April 11th, 2026.
There was an excellent turnout, with lots of interest shown from potential new members.

title: Green Maintenance
content: A huge thanks to Roger Miller and the grounds team for their tireless work over the winter.
The green is looking fantastic and ready for a busy 2026 season.
